input.headline: Ed Sheeran has already planned his posthumous album

raw.headline: Ed Sheeran has already planned his posthumous album

winner: I can't believe they've actually started working on Ed Sheeran's 'Living While Dead' album.

candidates.text: Why did Ed Sheeran create a pre-funeral playlist? Because he wanted to ensure the party didn't stop even after his funeral!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, playing on the idea of a pre-funeral playlist and Ed Sheeran's known love for music. However, it lacks some punch and could be more concise or clever."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran's posthumous album? I guess he's already thinking, 'Shape of Pallbearers to Come!'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on Ed Sheeran's album title, concise and surprising with a play on 'Shape of You' and pallbearers."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran reveals his secret for staying alive - he's just waiting for the release of his pre-mortem album!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the headline with clever wordplay and concise delivery."}

candidates.text: Why did Ed Sheeran's ghost produce a new album? Because he wanted something from beyond the grave-y!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the pun on 'beyond the grave-y,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity. It doesn't fully tie into the headline about planning a posthumous album, and the humor feels generic rather than surprising."}

candidates.text: I can't believe they've actually started working on Ed Sheeran's 'Living While Dead' album.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific, with a clever twist on the concept of a posthumous album."}
